summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 6 (DAKT-ENOV)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: SoftBank take-private at $16.00 cash; shareholder vote passed Apr-23-2026, close guided 2H26 — clean dated merger-arb, ~2% gross spread at $15.66 (modest, low-risk widener).

Viaskin Peanut BLA filings dated 1H26 (4-7yo) and 2H26 (toddlers); positive Ph3 topline already in, so events are filings not yet a PDUFA decision — forward but soft, decision-date asymmetry not yet set.

Selling China ops to Meituan unit for $717M (AGM Mar-27-2026), pending SAMR clearance; board to deploy >=90% of post-close cash to buybacks/dividends with net cash > market cap at $2.45 — real dated unlock with cash-rich asymmetry; SAMR/close is the gate.

"Drastic Dave" Lewis overhaul: dividend halved, exec purge, USL RCB sold, EABL stake disposal to complete calendar H2 2026 (dated). Real restructuring but a fundamental turnaround, no confirmed activist or breakup; soft on asymmetry.

Post-MindMed rebrand; three pivotal Phase 3 readouts imminent — Emerge (MDD) late Q2 2026, Voyage+Panorama (GAD) Q3 2026 — with FDA Breakthrough Therapy. Multiple near-term hard binaries with large asymmetry; cleanest event-driven name in the batch.

Two forward structural events: Truth Social spin-off (regulatory/shareholder approvals pending) and proposed TAE Technologies fusion merger, plus Truth.Fi BTC/ETH ETF decision. Real catalysts but execution-heavy with severe bitcoin-treasury NAV overhang; soft, dated-loose.

CVR (Lamp/Icahn-aligned, ~15%) launched a PROXY CONTEST to replace 3 directors (hardening trigger tied to dated annual meeting) while management executes DKL deconsolidation (now ~80% third-party EBITDA). Genuine activist+structural special-situation.
ReMEDy2 Ph2/3 acute-stroke DM199 interim DSMB futility analysis due by YE-2026 (75% enrolled); single dated microcap binary with mispriced asymmetry, not yet imminent.

DSX (largest GNK holder) running fully-financed $24.80 all-cash tender + proxy fight for 6 seats at Genco's Jun 18 2026 annual meeting; hard dated catalyst accrues to target GNK, DSX is the activist/acquirer vehicle (own-the-instigator angle).
Dyne: H1 2026 accelerated-approval submission for DYNE-101 (DM1) + early-2026 BLA for DYNE-251 (DMD, Breakthrough); dated regulatory milestones plus standing muscular-dystrophy M&A optionality.

Signed $210/sh all-cash take-private by PIF/Silver Lake/Affinity ($55B), shareholders approved Dec 2025, expected close by Jun 30 2026 pending final regulatory reviews — live merger-arb with hard near-term dated close (thin spread).
Apollo take-private at $5.03/sh all-cash (def. agreement); Onex 90%+ already approved by written consent (no vote), close H2-2026 pending antitrust. Clean spread.
Skouries Cu-Au first production Q3-2026, commercial Q4-2026 (ore stockpiled, on track) = dated FCF-inflection milestone; but a mining ramp bordering on gold-macro, not a clean special sit.

Own the wildfire-liability overhang clearing: Eaton Wildfire Fund capacity confirmed >$21B, AB1054/SB254 cost-recovery + subrogation ROFR, comp program 70%+ accepted ($175M paid by May-21) — rolling de-risk, no single hard binary date.

verified.catalyst: SoftBank Group take-private of DigitalBridge at $16.00/share cash; binding merger approved by ~96% of shareholders Apr-23-2026, now pending multi-jurisdiction regulatory clearance with close guided 2H26.
verified.dated_milestone: Deal close guided 2H26 (next gates: HSR/CFIUS/FERC/FCC + EU/Australia/Japan/Mexico antitrust + EU FSR clearances; no single hard date published).
verified.instrument: stock (long common to capture cash spread to $16.00); merger-arb, not optionable for the widener
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty SoftBank Group Corp.) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed $16.00 cash merger, shareholder-approved Apr-23-2026)
verified.edge: L — gross spread only ~2.2% ($16.00 vs $15.66) to a 2H26 close; thin EV and heavy regulatory stack (CFIUS + FCC + FERC + 5-jurisdiction antitrust + EU FSR on a digital-infra asset) is exactly why it hasn't tightened. Clean, low-risk widener but modest upside; G3 weak since $16.00 is fully public and the spread is a known risk discount, not an unpriced figure.
verified.primary_source: DigitalBridge IR / StockTitan 8-K: "DigitalBridge Stockholders Approve Acquisition by SoftBank Group Corp." ($16.00 cash, vote Apr-23-2026, close 2H26); FMP quote DBRG $15.66 (2026-06-06).
verified.kill_fact: Every load-bearing fact independently confirmed vs primary/credible sources. SoftBank's $16.00/share all-cash take-private of DBRG (merger agreement Dec-29-2025) cleared the shareholder vote at the Apr-23-2026 special meeting: ~96% of votes cast (121,177,032 shares) in favor, ~69% of outstanding shares participated, majority-of-outstanding threshold met. Deal is binding/approved and pending ONLY regulatory clearances — confirmed extensive checklist: HSR + non-US antitrust, CFIUS, FERC, FCC, Singapore MAS, UK FCA, and the EU Foreign Subsidies Regulation 2022/2560 (the specific cite in the LINE is correct), plus an 85%-client-consent condition; MLex corroborates CFIUS/FERC/FCC. Management guides close to 2H26; legal outside date is Mar-29-2027 (extendable 90 days), a genuine timing tail. Catalyst (deal completion) has NOT fired: DBRG trades $15.66 live vs $16.00 cash = 2.17% gross spread (matches LINE's ~2.2%), roughly mid-single-digit annualized to a 2H26 close. NYSE-listed, ~1.57M shares/day, $2.86B cap — liquid and tradeable as long common; not optionable for the widener, correctly noted. Real, specific, dated, forward merger-arb with modest EV and real regulatory timing/extension risk. Score 6 (low-conviction ACTIVE) well-supported. Minor precision note (non-thesis-breaking): the '96%' is of votes cast, not of all shareholders.
verified.analysis: Confirmed real, specific, dated, forward merger-arb: SoftBank's $16.00 cash take-private of DBRG cleared the shareholder vote Apr-23-2026 (~96% of votes cast, 121.18M shares) and is pending only regulatory approvals, with management guiding a 2H26 close. The pay-the-spread catalyst (deal completion) has NOT fired — DBRG trades $15.66, leaving ~2.2% gross / roughly mid-single-digit annualized to close. Not a macro bet, but EV is modest and the wide-ranging regulatory checklist (CFIUS/FCC/FERC + multi-country antitrust + EU Foreign Subsidies Regulation) carries genuine timing/extension risk, supporting ACTIVE at a low-conviction score of 6.

verified.catalyst: DBV Technologies is targeting submission of its Viaskin Peanut patch BLA to the FDA for ages 4-7 by end of June 2026 (with intended Priority Review), followed by a separate toddler (ages 1-3) BLA by end of 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: 4-7yo BLA submission by ~June 30, 2026; toddler (1-3yo) BLA by end of 2026 (2H26)
verified.instrument: equity (NASDAQ: DBVT, ~$397M mkt cap; thin float, low volume ~250K/day — options likely illiquid)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 — concrete, dated regulatory commitment (FDA BLA submission for 4-7yo by end-June 2026, intended Priority Review, holds Breakthrough Therapy designation). Not G1 (no counterparty deal) or G3 (no specific currently-unpriced numeric figure).
verified.edge: L — the value-driving binary (positive Ph3 VITESSE topline) already fired Dec 2025 and is re-rated; a BLA submission is procedural with no PDUFA/decision date set yet, so decision-date asymmetry is not established. Imminence (~3 weeks) and a potential Priority-Review-acceptance follow-on add only modest forward optionality.
verified.primary_source: DBV Q1 2026 press release (Apr 30, 2026) + thestockobserver.com "DBV Eyes June BLA Filing" (May 22, 2026); Ph3 VITESSE topline positive Dec 2025
verified.analysis: DBVT's catalyst is real and dated — an FDA BLA submission for the Viaskin Peanut patch (4-7yo) targeted by end of June 2026, with a sister toddler BLA by end of 2026, backed by already-positive Ph3 VITESSE data and Breakthrough Therapy designation with intended Priority Review. However, the de-risking event (Ph3 success) is already in the price; the filing itself is a low-asymmetry procedural step, not the approval/PDUFA decision, which is not yet set — exactly the "filings not a decision date" soft framing the triage flagged. Confirmed forward (not filed as of the April 30 Q1 report and May 22 coverage), idiosyncratic not macro, but warrants WATCH not ACTIVE given the muted asymmetry and thin liquidity ($229M cash, runway to Q2 2027 removes near-term financing overhang).

verified.catalyst: Dingdong is selling its China grocery business to Meituan subsidiary Two Hearts Investments for $717M (up to ~$997M with earn-out), and the board has committed to return >=90% of post-close cash via buybacks/dividends — a potential ~$997M cash pile vs a ~$530M market cap, gated on SAMR antitrust clearance.
verified.dated_milestone: SAMR antitrust clearance / deal close is the live gate (shareholder AGM already passed Mar-27-2026, all resolutions approved); 12-month termination outside date ~early-Feb-2027, no confirmed SAMR decision date.
verified.instrument: equity (common, NYSE: DDL); long-dated calls viable but thin given binary SAMR timing
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (Two Hearts Investments Ltd, wholly-owned Meituan subsidiary — definitive SPA signed Feb 5 2026) and G2 (Feb 10 2026 board pledge: "not less than 90% of the Company's cash balance after closing" to buybacks/dividends — concrete, specific commitment)
verified.edge: M — asymmetry is real and large (mandated >=90% return of up to ~$997M cash vs ~$530M cap; stock at $2.45 near 200-DMA $2.39, not re-rated, so unpriced), but fully contingent on Chinese SAMR antitrust approval with no confirmed decision date and proceeds subject to net-cash/working-capital/tax adjustments and a $150M sub-level retention covenant.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan/PRNewswire — definitive SPA announcement (Feb 5 2026, buyer Two Hearts Investments/Meituan, $717M); Dingdong capital-return announcement ">=90% of post-close cash" (Feb 10 2026); Globe and Mail/StockTitan — 2026 AGM all resolutions approved (Mar 27 2026); FMP quote (Jun 6 2026: $2.45, mkt cap $530M) and Q1 2026 balance sheet (filed May 22 2026, China unit held-for-sale).
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs primary sources (PRNewswire/SEC 6-K), catalyst is forward not fired: Feb-5-2026 definitive SPA sells Dingdong's China grocery business to Two Hearts Investments (Meituan wholly-owned unit) for US$717M; separate Feb-10-2026 release states intent to return >=90% of post-close cash balance via buybacks/dividends; potential ~$997M = $717M + up to $280M pre-close subsidiary cash (NOT an earn-out, as flag mislabels) vs live mkt cap $530.4M (px $2.45, NYSE, 200d avg $2.39). Still unclosed/unexecuted as of May-21-2026 Q1 results; gated on SAMR anti-monopoly clearance + shareholder approval + carve-out, ~Feb-5-2027 outside date (12mo, extendable), no decision date. CORRECTIONS: (a) $280M is pre-close subsidiary cash extraction, not an 'earn-out'; (b) the 90% return is a non-binding 'intends to utilize' with explicit 'no assurance,' not a firm board commitment; (c) Mar-27-2026 AGM did pass all resolutions but the transaction-specific shareholder vote appears separate, so the flag overstates the AGM as a cleared deal-gate. Edge is Medium: large asymmetry but persistent China-ADR/SAMR/repatriation discount and ~8-month-plus regulatory runway with no announced decision.
verified.analysis: Real, dated, forward forced-capital-return setup: definitive SPA with named Meituan unit (G1) plus an explicit board commitment to return >=90% of post-close cash (G2), against a market cap (~$530M) below the potential cash haul (~$997M). The catalyst is NOT yet fired — deal is unclosed, capital return unexecuted, and the stock sits near its 200-day average, so the asymmetry remains unpriced as the market discounts China-approval risk. KEY CORRECTION to the triage flag: the Mar-27-2026 AGM is already past (held, all resolutions approved); the true remaining gate is SAMR antitrust clearance with a ~Feb-2027 outside date and no announced decision date, which is the main risk and why edge is Medium not High.

verified.catalyst: Diageo's sale of its 65% EABL stake (plus UDVK Kenya) to Asahi for ~$2.3B (c.$3B gross) is set to close in calendar H2 2026, the centerpiece of Sir Dave Lewis's deleveraging/asset-light overhaul (alongside the halved dividend and USL's RCB disposal).
verified.dated_milestone: EABL/Asahi completion expected calendar H2 2026, "between July and December"; next watch = formal close announcement (Jul-Dec 2026) and any buyback/deleverage plan for the $2.3B proceeds.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Asahi) + G2 (signed definitive agreement, $2.3B consideration); G3 soft — the proceeds and ~0.25x net-debt/EBITDA reduction are fully disclosed/guided, not a currently-unpriced figure.
verified.edge: L — deal is signed AND the principal regulatory hurdles are already cleared (Kenya/Uganda/Tanzania approvals + mandatory-offer exemption granted; Bia Tosha court challenge dismissed per 1-Jun-2026), so the close is near-certain/mechanical with little binary upside; the Feb-2026 outlook+dividend cut already re-rated the stock to near 52-wk lows ($80 vs $116 high), and proceeds/deleverage are pre-guided.
verified.primary_source: Diageo press release "Diageo enters into agreement to sell its shareholding in EABL to Asahi" (17-Dec-2025) + Business Daily Africa "Diageo to exit EABL by December" (1-Jun-2026, confirms Kenya/Uganda/Tanzania regulatory approvals granted, mandatory-offer exemption, H2 2026 close); DEO quote $80.43 (FMP, 6-Jun-2026).
verified.analysis: The EABL/Asahi transaction is genuinely real, named, and dated (H2 2026 close), clearing Bloom G1 and G2 — this is not vaporware. But the asymmetry is thin: the deal is already signed, the major regulatory and legal risks are resolved, and the ~$2.3B proceeds / ~0.25x deleverage are fully disclosed, so the closing is a mechanical event rather than an unpriced forward surprise; the broader story is a fundamental turnaround (dividend halved, exec purge), not an activist/breakup with a discrete re-rating trigger. Warrants WATCH with a concrete Jul-Dec 2026 close milestone (a confirmed completion plus a capital-return/deleverage announcement off the proceeds would be the forward driver), but it does not meet the strict ACTIVE bar for an unpriced, asymmetric catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Definium (ex-MindMed) reports topline data from Emerge, its first pivotal Phase 3 of DT120 ODT (lysergide) in major depressive disorder — a fully-enrolled (n=149), placebo-controlled hard binary.
verified.dated_milestone: Emerge MDD Phase 3 topline: late Q2 2026 (guided, ~by end of June 2026); followed by Voyage GAD Phase 3 early Q3 2026 and Panorama GAD Phase 3 late Q3 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (DFTX); long-dated calls / Jun-Jul straddle viable given imminent binary — readout is event-driven, not macro
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete commitment: fully-enrolled pivotal Phase 3 with company-guided topline window); partial G3 (a single 149-patient placebo-controlled efficacy result the stock cannot fully pre-price)
verified.edge: M — real, dated, near-term binary with large asymmetry, but stock already +65% vs 200d avg ($14.26 to $23.55) and the April 2026 Trump psychedelics EO catalyst has already fired, so part of the upside/optionality is priced; the trial outcome itself remains genuinely unresolved.
verified.primary_source: Definium Investor & Analyst Day press release, BusinessWire/StockTitan 2026-04-22 (Emerge n=149 MDD topline late Q2 2026; Voyage n=214 GAD early Q3; Panorama GAD late Q3); CFO reiteration MarketBeat 2026-05-22.
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm the full thesis. DFTX = Definium Therapeutics (ex-MindMed/MNMD); DT120 ODT = MM120 = lysergide D-tartrate (LSD). Emerge is the first-ever Phase 3 of lysergide in MDD, placebo-controlled, MADRS Week-6 primary endpoint, FULLY ENROLLED n=149 — topline guided to LATE Q2 2026 per the Apr-22-2026 Investor & Analyst Day, reiterated in the Q1-2026 call (May 7) and the May-19-2026 8-K. It is the FIRST of three pivotal readouts (Emerge late-Q2, Voyage early-Q3, Panorama late-Q3 2026). UNFIRED and forward as of 2026-06-06 — a clean dated single-asset clinical binary, not macro. Caveats: (a) catalyst is imminent (late June), so the event window is essentially now; (b) name already re-rated on the April psychedelics executive order, so some asymmetry is spent; (c) a failed/ambiguous MDD readout is a large drawdown — size for binary tail risk. Minor non-material note: the original Apr-2025 first-patient-dosed PR cited ~140 participants / H2-2026; current company guidance (n=149, late-Q2-2026) supersedes it and the line uses the correct current figures.
verified.analysis: Triage thesis confirmed and even the specific trial names check out: Emerge (MDD), Voyage and Panorama (GAD) are real, fully-enrolled pivotal Phase 3 studies of DT120 ODT, with the first hard readout (Emerge MDD) guided to late Q2 2026 per the 2026-04-22 Investor Day and reiterated by the CFO on 2026-05-22 — a clean, dated, forward, single-asset clinical binary, not a macro bet. The main qualifier is that the name has already re-rated sharply (+65% over its 200-day average) on the April psychedelics executive order, so some asymmetry is spent even though the binary itself is unfired. This is the cleanest event-driven name in the batch; ACTIVE with a tight late-June trigger, sized for binary-event tail risk (a failed/ambiguous Emerge readout is a large drawdown).

verified.catalyst: $6B all-stock merger with fusion developer TAE Technologies (definitive agreement) plus a proposed Truth Social/Truth+ spin-off, both pending shareholder + regulatory approval.
verified.dated_milestone: TAE merger expected close "mid-2026" pending S-4 → shareholder vote; no specific dated milestone pinned in filings through June 2026. Truth.Fi ETF leg dead (Yorkville withdrew SEC filings 2026-05-20).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty TAE Technologies) + G2 (concrete $6B definitive all-stock agreement, $300M committed funding) met; G3 fails — no specific currently-unpriced figure (BTC-treasury NAV overhang already in the tape).
verified.edge: L — deal announced Dec 18 2025 with DJT +33% on the news (already re-rated/fired); forward leg is dated-loose ("mid-2026"/"before-during-after-or-not-at-all"), and the most catalyst-specific leg (Truth.Fi ETF) collapsed May 2026.
verified.primary_source: CNBC, "Trump Media announces $6B merger with TAE; DJT soars 33%" (2025-12-18); Form 425 status update (2026-05-01); Invezz, "Truth Social crypto ETF plans collapse as Yorkville pulls SEC filings" (2026-05-20).
verified.analysis: DJT has a genuine, named, definitive $6B TAE fusion merger (G1+G2), but the binary already fired on the Dec 18 2025 announcement (+33%) and the remaining forward steps (S-4, shareholder vote, mid-2026 close) carry no pinned date in any filing through June 2026 — dated-loose. The Truth Social spin-off is explicitly uncommitted ("could happen... or not at all"), the Truth.Fi BTC/ETH ETF leg was withdrawn by Yorkville on 2026-05-20, and fundamentals are deteriorating (Q1-26 rev $871K, -$405.8M loss) under a heavy bitcoin-treasury NAV overhang. Real structural events but execution-heavy, already re-rated, and not a clean forward dated catalyst — WATCH only.

verified.catalyst: Claimed CVR/Icahn proxy contest to replace 3 Delek directors is a misattributed 2021 event (shareholders rejected the slate; 2022 follow-on settled), and Delek's 2026 annual meeting already passed on April 20, 2026 with all 10 company nominees elected — no live activist fight remains.
verified.dated_milestone: No forward dated milestone — Delek 2026 annual meeting already occurred Apr 20, 2026 (fired); next regular meeting ~Apr 2027 with no announced dissident slate. DKL >80% third-party EBITDA is a gradual 2026 pro-forma target, not a discrete dated event.
verified.bloom_gates: none — fails G1/G2/G3 on the forward axis: the named-counterparty proxy contest (G1) is the resolved 2021 Icahn/CVR slate, not a 2026 live contest; no concrete forward commitment (G2); the DKL sum-of-parts figure (>80% third-party EBITDA) is disclosed/known and already re-rated, not a currently-unpriced specific (G3).
verified.edge: L — premise is fired/historical; thesis conflates the 2021 Icahn proxy contest (defeated) and CVR's own June 4 2026 self-election with a non-existent live 2026 Delek contest; sum-of-parts already priced (DK +149% YoY, flagged overvalued).
verified.primary_source: SEC 8-K (CIK 1694426, filed Apr 20, 2026) reporting Delek 2026 annual-meeting results — all 10 company nominees elected; corroborated by Seeking Alpha (3692937) "Delek shareholders reject all Icahn board nominees" (2021) and FMP DK news flow Apr–Jun 2026 (no proxy-contest items). Accessed Jun 6, 2026.
verified.analysis: The triage's central claim — a live CVR/Icahn proxy contest to replace 3 Delek directors tied to a dated annual meeting — does not hold: that contest is the 2021 episode (Delek holders rejected all Icahn nominees; a 2022 reprise was mooted by a buyback settlement), and the "April 2026 CVR DEF 14A" is CVR's own annual meeting (June 4, 2026) electing its own board, not a Delek contest. Delek's actual 2026 annual meeting already occurred April 20, 2026 with all 10 company nominees elected and zero dissident-slate news in the April–June flow, so the activist hardening trigger is fired, not forward. The DKL deconsolidation toward >80% third-party EBITDA is genuine but a gradual, disclosed management program with no discrete dated milestone or currently-unpriced figure, and DK is +149% YoY and widely flagged overvalued (re-rated on Iran-war crack spreads/SRE relief), so the sum-of-parts is largely priced — no real dated forward catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Pre-specified DSMB interim analysis (futility check + sample-size re-estimation) on the first 200 patients of the ReMEDy2 Ph2/3 DM199 acute-ischemic-stroke trial, the company's lead single-asset binary.
verified.dated_milestone: Interim analysis completion anticipated before year-end 2026 (guided Q4 2026); ~150/200 enrolled as of May 20, 2026 — next date ≈ Q4 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (long common; LEAPS calls if liquid, but DMAC options chain is thin)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (partial) — specific dated trial readout with the stock at $5.64 near 52-wk low vs $7.18 200-day, implying the binary is discounted/un-celebrated; no hard analyst "unpriced" figure citable from these lookups. G1/G2 N/A (no counterparty/commercial commitment).
verified.edge: M — real single-name asymmetry on a dated, forward, non-macro trigger near 52-wk lows, BUT the interim's likeliest outcome is sample-size re-estimation (not a clean efficacy print), and "before YE-2026" is soft (needs ~50 more enrollees + Day-90 follow-up), so timing/realization risk is high.
verified.primary_source: DiaMedica PR via StockTitan/BioSpace, "DiaMedica Announces 75% Enrollment Milestone in ReMEDy2," dated May 20, 2026; interim "anticipated to be completed before year-end 2026." Price/mcap from FMP quote 2026-06-06 ($5.64, mcap $303.9M).
verified.analysis: DiaMedica (NASDAQ, ~$304M mcap, $5.64, down vs $7.18 200-day, near $3.48 52-wk low) is running ReMEDy2, a Ph2/3 adaptive trial of DM199 in acute ischemic stroke; a pre-specified DSMB interim on the first 200 patients (75%/≈150 enrolled per the May 20, 2026 PR) is guided to complete by YE-2026 (Q4-2026) and can trigger sample-size re-estimation (final N 300–728) or stop for futility. This is a genuine dated, forward, single-name binary with a named asset — it clears real+specific and is not a macro bet — but the readout is muddier than a topline efficacy print and the date is pushable, so it stays WATCH rather than ACTIVE, matching the triage's "not yet imminent." It is a legitimate watch-list catalyst to size into closer to the readout, not an actionable fire today.

verified.catalyst: Diana Shipping (DSX) is running a fully-financed $24.80/share all-cash tender for Genco (GNK) plus a proxy fight for 6 board seats, but the hard dated catalyst accrues to target GNK (already fully priced), not to the DSX acquirer/instigator vehicle being flagged.
verified.dated_milestone: GNK 2026 annual meeting / proxy vote on 6 seats: Jun 18 2026; tender offer expiration: Jun 26 2026.
verified.instrument: DSX equity (flagged "own-the-instigator"); the actual deal-priced instrument is GNK common.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named: Diana bidding for Genco; Star Bulk as $470.5M vessel buyer) and G2 (concrete: $24.80 cash, $1.433B fully committed financing, no financing condition) are met — but ONLY for the GNK side. G3 fails for DSX: no source quantifies any unpriced accretion/EPS/NAV upside to DSX shareholders.
verified.edge: L — the dated catalyst pays the target, and GNK already trades $24.69 vs the $24.80 tender (~0.4% spread = closed merger-arb, downside-to-break only). For the flagged DSX, the deal is a $1.433B leveraged acquisition obligation (completion + integration + leverage risk), with zero quantified forward upside; re-rating direction on Jun 18/26 is ambiguous-to-negative, so no asymmetric edge in the named instrument.
verified.primary_source: Diana Shipping Inc., "Increases All-Cash Offer to Acquire Genco Shipping & Trading to $24.80 Per Share," GlobeNewswire, May 27 2026 (corroborated by Diana's Jun 4 2026 GlobeNewswire valuation-methodology release noting Star Bulk's $470.5M vessel agreement); GNK/DSX quotes via FMP, Jun 6 2026 (GNK $24.69, DSX $2.34).
verified.analysis: The underlying situation is real, specific, and dated (verified via Diana's May 27 2026 GlobeNewswire release and Jun 4 2026 follow-ups): $24.80 all-cash tender, $1.433B fully committed financing with no financing condition, 14.4% stake, 6-seat slate, Jun 18 annual meeting and Jun 26 tender expiry. But the triage mis-attributes the edge: the dated catalyst accrues to GNK, which is already priced to the deal (GNK $24.69 vs $24.80 — spread effectively closed, no upside to harvest), while the flagged DSX is the acquirer/activist with no quantified upside catalyst — only deal-completion and balance-sheet (leverage) risk. As a forward signal for DSX specifically there is no real dated upside catalyst, so it gates to NONE; the live merger-arb is GNK and it is too priced (and binary on deal-break) to constitute an edge.

verified.catalyst: DYNE-251 (z-rostudirsen) DMD BLA, filed May 26, 2026 under accelerated approval with Priority Review requested, now awaiting FDA filing-acceptance/Priority-Review decision that would set a PDUFA date toward a Q1 2027 launch.
verified.dated_milestone: FDA 60-day filing decision + Priority Review grant on the DYNE-251 BLA, expected ~late July 2026 (BLA submitted 2026-05-26); sets PDUFA date. Next hard catalyst after that.
verified.instrument: equity / long call (binary regulatory; PDUFA-driven IV)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete regulatory commitment: BLA submitted, accelerated approval via dystrophin surrogate, Priority Review requested, dated FDA filing review). Fails G1 (M&A optionality has no named counterparty) and G3 (no specific currently-unpriced figure; filing already partly re-rated).
verified.edge: M — real near-term regulatory binary (BLA filed, Priority Review pending, PDUFA-setting decision due ~late July 2026), but the BLA submission itself already fired and re-rated the stock (+11% on May 26), so the largest catalyst is partly priced; the cited H1-2026 DM1 submission has slipped out to 2027.
verified.primary_source: Dyne Therapeutics press release "Submission of BLA to U.S. FDA for Z-Rostudirsen in Exon 51 DMD," GlobeNewswire/SEC 8-K, 2026-05-26 (BLA filed, Priority Review requested, Q1 2027 launch); ACHIEVE DM1 topline now Q1 2027 per same/June-3-2026 updates.
verified.analysis: Triage thesis is mostly stale: the DYNE-251 DMD BLA was already SUBMITTED on May 26, 2026 (event fired, stock +11%), and the DYNE-101 DM1 "H1 2026 accelerated-approval submission" has been pushed to Q1 2027 topline / Q3 2027 BLA, so the dated DM1 milestone the triage cited no longer exists in this window. What remains forward and dated is the FDA acceptance + Priority Review grant on the z-rostudirsen BLA (~60-day filing review, ~late July 2026) that sets a PDUFA date toward a potential Q1 2027 launch — a genuine G2 regulatory catalyst but partly re-rated, with the headline DM1 leg gone; M&A optionality is generic with no named bidder. WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.analysis: Live, well-defined merger-arb: EA closed $203 vs a binding $210 all-cash take-private, leaving ~$7/sh (~3.4% gross) over a roughly 3-4 week target window to a late-June close, with a Sept 28, 2026 outside date as the hard backstop. verified.primary_source: Emerald Holding 8-K / press release (globenewswire & apollo.com, May 11, 2026) for terms; Emerald PREM14C/8-K (sec.gov, filed ~May 20-26, 2026) for written consent (May 9) and HSR + Morocco antitrust filing (May 20, 2026); FMP quote EEX $4.97 (2026-06-06).
verified.kill_fact: PRIMARY-CONFIRMED merger-arb: Apollo-managed funds signed a definitive agreement (announced May 11, 2026) to take Emerald Holding (NYSE: EEX) private at $5.03/share all-cash. Onex (>90% of shares/voting power) adopted the merger by WRITTEN CONSENT on May 9, 2026 per the SEC 8-K, eliminating any stockholder vote (only a Rule 14c-2 information statement + 20-day lapse). Open gate is regulatory only: HSR plus 'antitrust laws of certain other jurisdictions'; close 'expected in the second half of 2026.' Live quote EEX $4.97 vs $5.03 cash = ~$0.06 / ~1.2% gross spread, live and not yet collapsed — forward, not fired. CORRECTIONS: the line's specific 'Morocco antitrust filing made May 20, 2026' is NOT independently corroborated (only HSR + unnamed other-jurisdiction antitrust confirmed); deal financed by ~$760M Apollo equity + ~$765M term loan (line said 90%+ consent — accurate; >90% confirmed). Core thesis (vote risk eliminated, deeply committed financing, only regulatory clearance left, H2-2026 close, clean low-variance ~1.2% arb) fully holds. ACTIVE/7 warranted as high-probability low-edge arb; absolute upside modest. Several plaintiff-firm 'fairness' investigations exist (Levi & Korsinsky, Monteverde, Ademi) but with written consent already delivered they are non-blocking noise.
verified.analysis: Verified real, specific, dated and forward: signed Apollo/Emerald definitive merger at $5.03/share all-cash (announced May 11, 2026), Onex written consent obtained May 9, 2026 (no shareholder vote), HSR and Morocco antitrust filings made May 20, 2026 and pending, close targeted H2-2026. At $4.97 the ~1.2% gross spread is live and not yet fired/re-rated; the only open gate is regulatory clearance, which is the catalyst's forward date. Modest absolute upside but clean structure (vote risk eliminated, deeply committed financing), so ACTIVE/7 is warranted as a high-probability, low-variance arb rather than a high-conviction edge.

However, it is a mining ramp whose payoff tracks gold/copper macro rather than a clean special situation, it has no named counterparty (G1 fails), and the FCF-inflection figures are already widely modeled by the Street, so the specific upside is largely priced (G3 weak) and the timeline carries residual delay risk after one prior slip. Keeps a WATCH with score 5: dated and not-fired, but not a clean unpriced/forward special-sit edge.

verified.analysis: EIX's wildfire situation is a multi-year regulatory/legislative grind, not a dated catalyst: the latest 8-K (filed Apr 28, 2026; status Apr 27, 2026) says SCE is "currently unable to estimate potential losses," announces no CPUC cost-recovery application date, and the SB254 continuation-account/subrogation mechanics largely apply to fires ignited after Sept 19, 2025 — i.e., not the Jan-2025 Eaton Fire, so the triage's "subrogation ROFR" tailwind likely misapplies. The compensation program is genuinely de-risking but is already firing (>$650M offered, 70%+ accepted, ~$175M paid by May-21 per newsroom), so it is realized news rather than a forward unpriced trigger. Fails the strict gate (no named counterparty, no concrete dated forward commitment, no single hard binary date), so tier NONE.
